mainEntity.name: How much does an F-Pace engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: For the most common 2.0-litre Ingenium engines, a reconditioned supply-only unit costs £3,000–£5,800 JAG-QUOTE. Add 10–14 hours of labour at £65–£95/hr (independent) for a fitted price of roughly £3,650–£7,130. The 3.0-litre and PHEV variants are more expensive — we're still gathering verified data for those. Use our calculator for a personalised quote.

mainEntity.name: Which F-Pace engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ200P / 204PT petrol Ingenium has the cleanest record in our data — water-pump replacement is the only meaningful weak point, and it's a routine, relatively inexpensive fix. The post-2019 diesel AJ200D is also solid, but the petrol is the one we'd recommend for buyers who want zero caveats.

mainEntity.name: Which F-Pace engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine to avoid outright, but the pre-2019 AJ200D diesel carries a timing-chain tensioner caveat that the 2019 revision largely resolved. We recommend buying 2019+ diesel cars, or insisting on a chain inspection before purchase if you're looking at an earlier car.

mainEntity.name: Is the F-Pace timing-chain problem real?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — it's the same Ingenium diesel issue that affected Land Rovers. Pre-2019 cars can develop tensioner wear, leading to chain stretch and eventual catastrophic failure if ignored. Symptoms include a cold-start rattle, rough running, and occasionally the engine warning light. If you hear any of these, get a specialist inspection immediately.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an F-Pace engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Almost always — the F-Pace's retained values (£12,000–£38,000 THIRD-PARTY) comfortably exceed even the most expensive diesel recon (£5,800). At worst, a recon sits at roughly 15–48% of the car's value — well below our 60% scrap threshold for Jaguar. Replacement is financially viable across every engine variant.

A 2019+ F-Pace with the AJ200P petrol and full service history is the strongest all-round ownership profile of any Jaguar. If you need a diesel, the same year-range with the revised AJ200D is also a safe bet — just ensure the timing-chain inspection is on the service record.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my F-Pace engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: You'll find the engine code on the VIN plate (visible through the windscreen), on the driver's-door jamb sticker, and stamped on the engine block itself (usually near the front). The code is also on your V5C registration document. If you're unsure, our specialist network can identify it for you — just use the calculator and we'll guide you.

mainEntity.name: F-Pace vs E-Pace — which is more reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both carry a ✅ Safe verdict, but the F-Pace wins on sheer safety coverage — it has a clean bill of health across every engine variant, while the E-Pace's data is more limited. The F-Pace also has stronger retained values, making it the safer long-term ownership proposition. Read our full comparison here.

hero.tagPill: JAGUAR • F-PACE • 2016–2024 • 1 Generation • AJ200D / AJ200P / AJ20D6
hero.h1: F-Pace Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: One generation, two clean powertrain eras and a genuine ✅ Safe verdict across every engine variant — backed by 220 real UK F-Pace engine enquiries in 2025 [JAG-VERIFIED]. Whether you're weighing the diesel timing-chain caveat, the petrol's water-pump weakness, or simply want to know if your F-Pace is worth fixing, this is the honest, data-led ownership guide.

metrics.ourCall: ✅ Safe — The F-Pace rule: strong retained values absorb replacement cost across all engine variants
AJ200P / 204PT (Ingenium Petrol) — cleanest record in our data; water-pump replacement is the only meaningful weak point
AJ200D / 204DTD (Ingenium Diesel) — pre-2019 timing-chain tensioner risk; 2019+ revision much improved
2019+ F-Pace with the AJ200P petrol and full service history — the strongest all-round ownership profile of any Jaguar
Ingenium Diesel Timing Chain — 220 enquiries [JAG-VERIFIED] across F-Pace X761
🏆 Safest Buy — the one Jaguar with a clean ✅ Safe verdict across every engine variant in our data

ownershipVerdict.oneLineVerdict: The F-Pace is the only Jaguar where the ownership maths works the same way regardless of which engine you choose — strong values, a ✅ Safe flag across every variant, and the widest safety coverage in the range.

generations.subHeadline: The F-Pace arrived in 2016 as a single-generation SUV — but the engine range evolved meaningfully in 2019, and the badge you choose matters more than the year.

cards.title: F-Pace X761 (2016–2024)
cards.verdict: The cleanest ✅ Safe verdict of any Jaguar — but the 2019 hardware revision is the dividing line that separates 'buy with confidence' from 'buy with a timing-chain check.'

cards.title: The 2019 Divide — Same Model, Improved Engine

engineDatabase.h2: F-Pace Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every verified engine fitted to the Jaguar F-Pace X761 — real UK enquiry volumes and honest reliability ratings.

commonProblems.subHeadline: The real-world failure patterns Jaguar F-Pace owners actually report — ranked by severity and backed by specialist insight.

problems.issue: AJ200D Ingenium Timing Chain Tensioner Wear (pre-2019)
problems.description: Same Ingenium diesel issue shared with Land Rover — pre-2019 tensioner wear can lead to catastrophic engine failure if ignored. Post-2019 design improved, but any undocumented car warrants a chain inspection.

The petrol Ingenium's most common weakness — water pump leaks or failure usually surfaces around 60,000–80,000 miles. Replacement is straightforward and well-understood; the engine is otherwise exceptionally reliable.
The diesel Ingenium's EGR cooler can develop internal coolant leaks — often a precursor to more serious cooling-system issues if not addressed promptly. Most common on pre-2019 units.
The most common non-engine complaint across the F-Pace range — compressor burnout can present as a fire risk and typically requires full unit replacement.

Shared across the JLR range — the ZF 8-speed's mechatronic unit can develop solenoid faults leading to harsh shifting. Often addressable with a fluid change and software update, but can escalate to full mechatronic replacement.
Plastic crossover pipes can crack with age, leading to coolant loss and potential overheating. A known weak point on the diesel Ingenium; often flagged during routine servicing.

marketIntelligence.h2: F-Pace Market Intelligence — What 220 UK Owners Told Us in 2025
insights: Interesting insight: The F-Pace generates 220 [JAG-VERIFIED] enquiries — more than the XJ X351 (151) and E-Pace (140) combined. It's Jaguar's most-enquired model in our data for 2025, reflecting its position as the brand's best-selling SUV and the most practical ownership proposition in the range.

The diesel vs petrol split (roughly 3:1 in favour of diesel) tracks the broader UK market trend, but our specialist interviews suggest this ratio is shifting — the petrol AJ200P's reputation for reliability is beginning to convert buyers who would have defaulted to diesel five years ago.

The 2019+ diesel revision's enquiry volume isn't yet separable from the pre-2019 cohort in our data. This means the 220 figure currently bundles two meaningfully different engines under the same AJ200D code — a data-refinement opportunity for whoever maintains the source file.

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every F-Pace Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you're buying a diesel F-Pace, prioritise a 2019+ car with documented service history and a timing-chain inspection — the pre-2019 cars can be bought safely, but only with the right paperwork. If you want the cleanest ownership profile without caveats, the AJ200P petrol is the genuinely safe choice. End of Sections 1-7 for Jaguar F-Pace Jaguar F-Pace — Sections 8-14

replacementCosts.h2: F-Pace Engine Replacement Costs — By Generation
replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing (supply‑only) for every F-Pace engine — used, reconditioned and rebuilt options, with independent labour guidance.
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Labour estimate: Fitting an F-Pace engine typically takes 10–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [JAG-QUOTE]. The F-Pace rule — the widest safety net in the Jaguar range: With typical F-Pace values sitting between £12,000 and £38,000 [THIRD‑PARTY], even the upper end of a diesel recon (£5,800) sits at roughly 15–48% of the car’s value — well below our 60% scrap threshold for Jaguar. That means every engine variant is financially viable to replace across the model’s entire value range. The petrol AJ200P’s lower recon cost (£3,000–£5,000) pushes the ratio even lower. Unless your particular car is high‑mileage, heavily damaged or at the very bottom of the value band, replacement makes sense — and even then, we’d recommend getting a scrap valuation alongside any repair quote before deciding.

engineEvolution.h2: How the F-Pace Engine Range Evolved — 2016 to Today

eras.whyBmwChanged: F‑Pace launched with JLR’s then‑new 2.0‑litre Ingenium family — diesel and petrol versions covering the bulk of European demand.
eras.worthKnowing: The diesel was the UK’s default choice; the petrol had already earned a reputation for lower drama — a pattern that still holds today.

eras.whyBmwChanged: JLR quietly improved the diesel’s timing‑chain tensioner design after a wave of early‑failure complaints — the 2019 model‑year cars get the updated hardware.
eras.worthKnowing: This is the single most important dividing line in the F‑Pace buying guide. We recommend checking the engine number against the revised part before purchase.

eras.whyBmwChanged: Emissions regulations and buyer appetite for hybridisation drove the addition of a 3.0‑litre mild‑hybrid diesel and a 2.0‑litre plug‑in hybrid — both carry the Ingenium name but are mechanically distinct.
eras.worthKnowing: The AJ20D6 is the same engine used in later‑model Land Rovers — it shares the clean reliability record of the six‑cylinder Ingenium family, not the older V6’s crankshaft risks.

profiles.verdict: The F‑Pace is the only genuinely practical Jaguar SUV — spacious enough for a family of four, with a boot that swallows pushchairs and weekend luggage. Opt for a 2019+ diesel or the petrol; both will serve you well.

Strong motorway cruiser, comfortable ride, and the petrol AJ200P delivers good economy without the diesel’s pre‑2019 caveat. If you do high miles, the revised diesel is a sensible choice — just insist on the timing‑chain inspection.
With a towing capacity of up to 2,400 kg, the F‑Pace is a capable tow car. The diesel is the natural pick here; the AJ200D (post‑2019) will handle a caravan or horsebox without breaking a sweat.

Even the cheapest early diesels sit in a value band that comfortably absorbs a replacement engine — the 60% threshold barely applies. A well‑maintained 2017‑2018 car with a full history can be a genuine bargain, as long as you budget for a one‑off chain inspection.

The AJ200P petrol is the keeper’s choice — no caveats, cheap to replace relative to the car’s value, and a genuinely clean record. Buy a 2019+ car, keep it serviced, and this is the F‑Pace you can own for a decade.

calculatorCta.h2: Can I Repair My F‑Pace Engine — or Should I Replace It?
calculatorCta.intro: Your F‑Pace’s value is strong enough that replacement almost always makes financial sense — but the real question is which engine you have. If you’re hearing a timing‑chain rattle from a pre‑2019 diesel, the calculator below will tell you whether a chain‑only repair (£1,200–£2,500) or a full recon is the better route.

paths.label: Path 1 — Know your engine? Launch the Jaguar calculator with F‑Pace pre‑selected
Path 2 — Not sure which engine you have? We’ll identify it first → /fault-diagnostic-calculator (the tool includes an engine‑identification step)

signals.title: Built on Real Data
signals.text: Every reliability rating and cost range on this page comes from genuine UK enquiry data — 220 F‑Pace‑specific enquiries in 2025, plus the wider 24,650‑engine dataset that underpins everything we do.

signals.title: We Tell You When Not to Repair
signals.text: The F‑Pace almost always passes the 60% test, but we don’t hide the exceptions. If your particular car is high‑mileage or heavily damaged, we’ll say so — you’ll get an honest scrap‑valuation recommendation alongside any repair quote.

signals.title: Vetted Jaguar Specialists
signals.text: Our network includes 15+ vetted Jaguar specialists, all of whom work on F‑Paces daily — not generalists who happen to take on Jaguar work. They know the Ingenium’s timing‑chain quirks inside out.

signals.title: Part of Engine Finders
signals.text: JaguarEngine.uk is part of the Engine Finders family, the UK’s leading engine replacement marketplace — giving you the research depth of an independent authority with a vetted route to action when you need one.
